title: Getting Started with browser-search — A Practical Guide for Frontend Developers
description: Step-by-step guide to getting started with browser-search. Learn setup, configuration, and best practices for Frontend.

sourceData.name: browser-search
sourceData.description: A skill for AI agents: search the web with SearXNG, browse with Camofox, bypass protections with CloakBrowser. Anti-hallucination by design. Self-hosted, free, unlimited.

bodyHtml:
browser-search is making waves in the Frontend ecosystem this week, gaining 4 new stars. With a total of 187 stars on GitHub and 20 forks, this open-source project is rapidly becoming a go-to tool for developers worldwide.

⭐ 187Stars
📈 +4/wkGrowth
🔀 20Forks
⚠️ 0Open Issues

Key Features

  • Written in JavaScript for maximum performance
  • Licensed under MIT — free to use and modify
  • 🏷️ agent-skills
  • 🏷️ ai-agent
  • 🏷️ ai-tools
  • 🏷️ browser-automation
  • 🏷️ camoufox

Why browser-search Is Trending

The Frontend landscape is constantly evolving, and browser-search has emerged as one of the fastest-growing projects this week. Several factors contribute to its momentum:

  1. Growing community: With 4 new stars this week alone, developer adoption is accelerating rapidly.
  2. Active maintenance: The project sees regular updates, ensuring compatibility with the latest ecosystem changes.
  3. Practical utility: A skill for AI agents: search the web with SearXNG, browse with Camofox, bypass protections with CloakBrowser. Anti-hallucination by design. Self-hosted, free, unlimited.

If you're working in Frontend, this is definitely a project worth watching — and adopting.

Practical Use Cases

Here are some common scenarios where browser-search shines:

  1. New projects: Start your next JavaScript project with this tool as a foundation
  2. Migration: Consider switching if your current solution is slowing you down
  3. Learning: Study the source code to understand modern Frontend patterns

Quick Start

Get started with browser-search in minutes:

# Clone the repository git clone https://github.com/Johell1NS/browser-search # Navigate to project cd browser-search # Check the README for detailed setup instructions

For full documentation and advanced usage, visit the official GitHub repository.

Final Thoughts

browser-search is a solid addition to any developer's toolkit. With its growing community, active development, and practical design, it's well worth your time to evaluate. Star it on GitHub, try it in a side project, and see if it fits your workflow.
